title: grisebachia ciliaris subsp. ciliciiflora
intro: Grisebachia ciliaris subsp. ciliciiflora, also known as the bushy club fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Grisebachia ciliaris subsp. ciliciiflora can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to a lack of extensive scientific research.
